title: Translate sheet labels/instructions per locale
context: Sheet labels and instructions (e.g. 'Trace the letters', 'Copy the letter', column/row headers) are currently in English. Non-English-speaking educators and learners — particularly those in refugee, migrant, and mother-tongue education programs that are core beneficiaries — need these labels in their own language to use the sheets effectively. Each locale translation requires a language reviewer to ensure pedagogical accuracy, not just fluency. The set of locales is not yet enumerated; this task represents the translation work for any confirmed locale.
objective: Translate sheet labels, instructions, and UI text into at least one confirmed non-English locale, with a language reviewer verifying pedagogical accuracy, and integrate the translations into the render pipeline so sheets can be generated with localized labels.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one non-English locale (specific locale to be confirmed by partner/educator need and reviewer availability) fully translated: all sheet labels, template instructions, and UI text strings.
Language reviewer (fluent speaker with pedagogical context, not machine translation alone) verifies the translations for accuracy and appropriate register for the target learner age group.
Translations integrated into the render pipeline: sheets can be generated with localized labels by passing a locale parameter.
Translation strings stored in a structured, localizable format (e.g. JSON locale files) that supports adding further locales without code changes.
Translated sheets verified to render correctly (text fits within label areas, no overflow or clipping).
resources: PLAN.md section 2 (Beneficiaries — refugee, migrant, mother-tongue education programs)
templates-010 (sheet templates containing label/instruction strings to translate)
output: Locale JSON translation files committed to the repo, integrated into the render pipeline, and verified by a language reviewer — with at least one complete non-English locale — licensed CC-BY-4.0.
